programme.desc: A look back at the weekend's action in the Premier League, featuring all of the goals, news and major talking points from around the top flight.

programme.title: Premier League Review
programme.desc: A look back at the weekend's action in the Premier League, featuring all of the goals, news and major talking points from around the top flight.

programme.title: PL: Bournemouth v Burnley Hlts
programme.desc: As those above them continue ticking over, what can Burnley do on the South Coast to improve their chances of Premier League survival? Scott Parker takes his side to Bournemouth. (20.12)

programme.title: PL: Brighton v Sunderland Hlts
programme.desc: Sunderland follow a derby day to remember with a visit to Brighton. The hosts, three points adrift of Regis Le Bris' side, lost on the road against Liverpool last weekend. (20.12)

programme.title: PL: Man City v West Ham Hlts
programme.desc: Hitting their stride at a pivotal time in the season, Manchester City are back at the Etihad to take on West Ham. City can temporarily leapfrog leaders Arsenal with a win. (20.12)

programme.title: PL: Wolves v Brentford Hlts
programme.desc: Wolves, desperately unlucky in the capital last week, face Brentford in the Premier League. Jordan Henderson netted for the Bees in his side's one-all draw with Leeds previously. (20.12)

programme.title: SNF: Spurs v Liverpool
programme.desc: A big test awaits for Thomas Frank and Spurs after a limp display against Forest as the champions Liverpool, looking to go six unbeaten, visit the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. (20.12)

programme.title: SNF: Everton v Arsenal Hlts
programme.desc: Lining up at the Hill Dickinson Stadium for the last time this year, Everton host Arsenal on Saturday Night Football. The Gunners needed two own goals to beat Wolves last weekend. (20.12)

programme.title: SNF: Leeds v Crystal Palace Hlts
programme.desc: On Saturday Night Football, Leeds attempt to extend an unbeaten Premier League run stretching three games with high-flying Crystal Palace the visitors at Elland Road. (20.12)

programme.title: PL: Aston Villa v Man Utd Hlts
programme.desc: Villa target a 10th consecutive win in all competitions on Super Sunday as the focus turns to Manchester United, who come from an exhilarating eight-goal thriller earlier this week. (21.12)

programme.title: MNF: Fulham v Nott'm Forest Hlts
programme.desc: Lifting themselves clear of the drop zone with a 3-0 win over Spurs, it's Fulham at Craven Cottage next on Monday Night Football for Sean Dyche's ever-improving Nottingham Forest. (22.12)

programme.title: FNF: Man Utd v Newcastle
programme.desc: Friday Night Football comes from Old Trafford as Manchester United take on Newcastle in the Premier League's sole Boxing Day fixture. This time last year, the Magpies won here 2-0. (26.12)

programme.title: PL: West Ham v Fulham
programme.desc: Searching for a much-needed win, West Ham host in-form London rivals Fulham. The Hammers sit in the drop zone with just 11 points to their name following a 3-0 defeat at City. (27.12)

programme.title: PL: Brentford v Bournemouth
programme.desc: Coming from a 2-0 win at Molineux, with two goals from Keane Lewis-Potter, Brentford take on Bournemouth, whose winless run stretched to eight matches previously. (27.12)

programme.title: PL: Burnley v Everton
programme.desc: Having ended a seven-game losing run to claim a precious point on the South Coast, can Burnley add three this time with the Premier League visit of Everton to Turf Moor? (27.12)

programme.title: PL: Arsenal v Brighton
programme.desc: Spending Christmas at the summit, title hopefuls Arsenal take on a Brighton side winless in their last four matches. Viktor Gyokores netted for the Gunners in a win over Everton. (27.12)

programme.title: PL: Liverpool v Wolves
programme.desc: Bringing three points home from the capital, but at the cost of injury to their record signing, the champions Liverpool welcome Wolves to Anfield in the Premier League. (27.12)

programme.title: SNF: Chelsea v Aston Villa Hlts
programme.desc: It's a Christmas cracker at Stamford Bridge as the Premier League's form team Aston Villa - seven points clear of Enzo Maresca's Chelsea - target an incredible 11th straight win. (27.12)

programme.title: PL: Sunderland v Leeds Hlts
programme.desc: Four unbeaten in the top flight, including a 4-1 demolition of Palace, Leeds go to Sunderland on Super Sunday. The Black Cats earned a point on the road against Brighton last week. (28.12)

programme.title: PL: Crystal Palace v Spurs Hlts
programme.desc: For the final time this year, Crystal Palace and Tottenham Hotspur take to the pitch for Premier League action as the two London rivals meet at Selhurst Park on Super Sunday. (28.12)

programme.title: Premier League Season Review
programme.desc: A review of the 2025-26 Premier League campaign.

programme.desc: Relive the action from the Premier League in a unique way with fast-paced highlights using alternative angles, featuring every goal from every game.

programme.title: PL Stories: Pochettino's Spurs
programme.desc: Ahead of the World Cup, revisit the rise of current USA manager Mauricio Pochettino's Tottenham; the team that transformed Spurs from top-four hopefuls into genuine challengers.

programme.title: Transfer 360: John Obi Mikel
programme.desc: John Obi Mikel signed a contract with Manchester United, but ended up moving to Chelsea. Get the inside story of one of the Premier League's most controversial transfer deals.

programme.title: Gary Neville's Soccerbox
programme.desc: Gary Neville joins legendary Premier League striker Les Ferdinand to reminisce about his greatest games and goals for QPR, Newcastle and Spurs.

programme.title: Gary Neville's Soccerbox
programme.desc: Gary Neville is joined by Duncan Ferguson to relive the games and goals that made him an Everton legend and one of the most feared strikers in Premier League history.

programme.title: Premier League Years 2002/03
programme.desc: Enjoy all of the most memorable moments from the 2002-03 Premier League season, accompanied by hit music from the time, major news stories and more.

programme.title: PL Retro: Man Utd v Man City 2009
programme.desc: A chance to look back over a classic Manchester derby as Man United welcome Man City to Old Trafford back in the 2009-10 season for a game that would go right to the wire.

programme.title: PL Greatest:A Villa/Birmingham '08
programme.desc: Aston Villa: Enjoy the greatest Premier League game for each of the league's 49 clubs, as voted for by fans. For Aston Villa, the 5-1 hammering of rivals Birmingham City back in April 2008.

programme.title: PL Greatest:Liverpool/Blackburn'95
programme.desc: Blackburn: Enjoy the greatest Premier League game for each of the league's 47 clubs, as voted for by fans. For Blackburn Rovers, the 2-1 loss to Liverpool that saw Rovers clinch the title.

programme.title: PL Greatest: L'Pool/Blackpool 2010
programme.desc: Blackpool: Enjoy the greatest Premier League game for each of the league's 47 clubs, as voted for by fans. Here, for Blackpool, the 2-1 win at Anfield against Liverpool in October 2010.

programme.title: PL Greatest: Bolton/Chelsea 2005
programme.desc: Chelsea: Enjoy the greatest Premier League game for the league's 49 clubs, as voted for by fans. For Chelsea, the win over Bolton in 2005 that won the Blues their first title in 50 years.

programme.title: Best Premier League Goals 20/21
programme.desc: A selection of incredible strikes during the 2020-2021 Premier League season, including stunners from Mohamed Salah, Mason Mount, Kevin De Bruyne and many more.

programme.title: PL Hat-Tricks: Alan Shearer
programme.desc: Remember the best hat-tricks from some of the Premier League's top goal scorers. This episode looks at the prolific Alan Shearer, who has bagged a joint-record 11 hat-tricks.

programme.title: PL Hat-Tricks: Sergio Aguero
programme.desc: Remember the best hat-tricks from some of the Premier League's top goal scorers. This episode covers Manchester City star Sergio Aguero, who has scored a joint-record 11 hat-tricks.

programme.title: Best PL Goals: Newcastle
programme.desc: Enjoy all of the best goals scored over the years by Newcastle United in the Premier League, including classic strikes by Alan Shearer, Laurent Robert and others.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:Spurs/Arsenal'04
programme.desc: Remember a classic match from the Premier League. Here, Arsenal head to White Hart Lane back in 2004, looking to secure the Premier League title.

programme.title: PL Greatest:A Villa/B'Ham City '08
programme.desc: A chance to relive a Premier League classic. Here, Aston Villa enjoy a 5-1 win over Midlands rivals Birmingham at Villa Park back in 2008.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games: Char v WHam '01
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic clash from the Premier League. Here, rewind to 2001 and thrilling encounter between Charlton Athletic and West Ham United at The Valley.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games: WHam v ManU '16
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic clash from the Premier League. Here, rewind to 2016 and a great game between West Ham and Manchester United at Upton Park.

programme.title: Premier League Review
programme.desc: A look back at the weekend's action in the Premier League, featuring all of the goals, news and major talking points from around the top flight.

programme.title: Premier League Review
programme.desc: A look back at the weekend's action in the Premier League, featuring all of the goals, news and major talking points from around the top flight.

programme.title: PL: Bournemouth v Chelsea Hlts
programme.desc: It's Bournemouth next for Enzo Maresca's Chelsea after defeat at Elland Road. The Cherries' poor Premier League run of form continued with a midweek defeat at home to Everton. (06.12)

programme.title: PL: Everton v Nott'm Forest Hlts
programme.desc: Everton and Nottingham Forest both bring three wins in their last four to this Premier League clash at the Hill Dickinson Stadium. Forest boss Sean Dyche returns to his former club. (06.12)

programme.title: PL: Man City v Sunderland Hlts
programme.desc: Regis Le Bris' Sunderland remain on their travels with another tough test, this time at the Etihad Stadium as hosts City look to maintain pace with the league leaders Arsenal. (06.12)

programme.title: PL: Newcastle Utd v Burnley Hlts
programme.desc: Denied a third straight win in the Premier League for the first time since April, Eddie Howe's Newcastle attempt to bounce back from a disappointing draw here against Burnley. (06.12)

programme.title: PL: Spurs v Brentford Hlts
programme.desc: Brought some breathing rooms thanks to captain Cristian Romero's acrobatics, Thomas Frank and Spurs target all three points as Brentford arrive at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. (06.12)

programme.title: SNF: Leeds v Liverpool Hlts
programme.desc: Two under-pressure managers Daniel Farke, whose Leeds side stepped up midweek to beat Chelsea, and Arne Slot go at it on Saturday Night Football with Leeds hosting Liverpool. (06.12)

programme.title: PL: Brighton v West Ham Hlts
programme.desc: Beaten by Aston Villa here in a seven-goal midweek thriller, Fabian Hurzeler's Brighton go again at the Amex Stadium with the Super Sunday visit of West Ham in the Premier League. (07.12)

programme.title: PL: Fulham v Crystal Palace Hlts
programme.desc: Fulham are back at Craven Cottage, the scene of a miraculous nine-goal classic midweek, for the visit of London rivals Crystal Palace in the Premier League on Super Sunday. (07.12)

programme.title: MNF: Wolves v Man Utd Hlts
programme.desc: Now the owners of the joint-worst record in Premier League history after 14 games played, winless Wolves entertain Ruben Amorim's Manchester United on Monday Night Football. (08.12)

programme.title: PL: Chelsea v Everton Hlts
programme.desc: Without a Premier League win in three, Chelsea need a result against Everton at Stamford Bridge. Three points here for the Toffees would see David Moyes' side leapfrog the Blues. (13.12)

programme.title: PL: Liverpool v Brighton Hlts
programme.desc: Having silenced the noise with an assured win at the San Siro midweek, Arne Slot's champions Liverpool return to Anfield for the Premier League visit of Fabian Hurzeler's Brighton. (13.12)

programme.title: SNF: Burnley v Fulham Hlts
programme.desc: Saturday Night Football comes from Turf Moor as Scott Parker's struggling Burnley host Fulham. The Clarets have a five-point gap to close in their fight for Premier League survival. (13.12)

programme.title: PL: West Ham v Aston Villa Hlts
programme.desc: Unai Emery's flying Villa, fresh from seeing off the title favourites, visit West Ham this Super Sunday. The Irons narrowly missed out on three points last weekend at Brighton. (14.12)

programme.title: PL: Nott'm Forest v Spurs Hlts
programme.desc: Two useful wins in two and Spurs are back on track as Thomas Frank brings his team to the City Ground for a Premier League clash on Super Sunday against Sean Dyche's Forest. (14.12)

programme.title: PL: Crystal Palace v Man City Hlts
programme.desc: Capitalising on the leaders' slip up last week, Manchester City go to Selhurst Park next looking to reaffirm their title credentials against a Palace side dreaming of the top four. (14.12)

programme.title: PL: Sunderland v Newcastle Hlts
programme.desc: After nine years, the Wear-Tyne derby makes its long-awaited Premier League return on Super Sunday as the Stadium of Light stages the 155th meeting of Sunderland and Newcastle. (14.12)

programme.title: PL: Brentford v Leeds Hlts
programme.desc: Daniel Farke's spirited Leeds arrive at the Gtech Community Stadium for a Super Sunday encounter with Keith Andrews' Brentford, who are four points better off than the visitors. (14.12)

programme.title: MNF: Man Utd v Bournemouth Hlts
programme.desc: It's another Monday Night Football Premier League assignment for Ruben Amorim and Manchester United, who bid to take another stride forward against an out-of-form Bournemouth. (15.12)
